facts.text: Samuel de Champlain founded Quebec City in 1608, choosing the Algonquin name Kébec, which means 'where the river narrows' at the Saint Lawrence.
The Battlefields Park in Quebec City houses 50 historic artillery pieces and marks the Plains of Abraham, the 1759 battlefield that shifted North American power.
Upper and lower Quebec City are connected by the Old Quebec Funicular and steep stairs like Escalier « casse‑cou », echoing the city’s medieval streets.
The Citadelle of Quebec, a National Historic Site on the Plains of Abraham, remains a living military fortress overlooking the Saint Lawrence.
Fort Charlesbourg‑Royal, founded by Jacques Cartier in 1541, was Quebec City’s first French settlement, abandoned after a harsh winter and indigenous resistance.
A Quebec City Tramway project is underway, aiming to connect the historic Old Quebec with modern districts and reduce traffic congestion.
